title: rubus koehleri subsp. pygmaeus
intro: Rubus koehleri subsp. pygmaeus, also known as the dwarf Korean raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus koehleri plant, a member of the rose family that is native to East Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus koehleri subsp. pygmaeus include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
